info.title: Global Picklists
info.description: Global sets are collections of picklist values that can be associated with multiple picklists across modules. Example use cases include maintaining a consistent set of status values or priority levels across different modules in a CRM system. By using global sets, administrators can ensure uniformity and simplify the management of picklist values, as any updates to the global set automatically reflect in all associated picklists. This feature enhances data integrity and reduces redundancy, making it easier to manage and analyze data across various modules.

get.description: Fetches the list of global picklists available in the organization along with their details. pick_list_values, used_in_modules, and associated_fields_count can be included in the response based on the 'include' query parameter. A 204 is returned when no global picklists exist.

200.description: Fetches the list of global picklists available in the organization along with their details. pick_list_values, used_in_modules, and associated_fields_count can be included in the response based on the 'include' query parameter. A 204 is returned when no global picklists exist.

schema.description: Response object containing an array of global picklists.

global_picklists.description: Array of global picklists with their properties.

display_label.description: Display label of the global picklist.

created_time.description: Creation timestamp of the global picklist.

modified_time.description: Last modification timestamp of the global picklist.

actual_label.description: Actual label of the global picklist.

associated_fields_count.description: Number of fields associated with this global picklist.

modified_by.description: User who last modified the global picklist.

oneOf.description: User object with name and id.
Null if no user information is available.

used_in_modules.description: Array of modules using this global picklist.

created_by.description: User who created the global picklist.

oneOf.description: User object with name and id.
Null if no user information is available.

customizable.description: Indicates if the global picklist is customizable.

pick_list_values_sorted_lexically.description: Indicates if picklist values are sorted lexically.

type.description: Type of the picklist value (used or unused).

sequence_number.description: Sequence number of the picklist value.

post.description: Creates a new global picklist with the specified display label, API name, description, and picklist values. The actual_label, customizable, modified_by, created_by, and presence properties are generated by the backend. In pick_list_values, only display_value and type are considered.

display_label.description: Display label of the global picklist.

pick_list_values_sorted_lexically.description: Indicates if picklist values should be sorted lexically.

pick_list_values.description: Array of picklist values. Only display_value and type are considered.

type.description: Type of the picklist value (used or unused).

schema.description: Response object for successful creation of a global picklist.

global_picklists.description: Array containing the created global picklist response.

details.description: Details of the created global picklist.

patch.summary: Update a global picklist by id
patch.description: Updates an existing global picklist identified by the path parameter \{id\}. You can update display_label, api_name, description, pick_list_values_sorted_lexically, and pick_list_values. Backend-generated properties (actual_label, customizable, modified_by, created_by, presence) cannot be updated. For pick_list_values, you can add new values, update existing values (using id), move values between used/unused (one at a time), or delete values (one at a time using _delete: true). Bulk operations for moving to unused or deletion are not allowed.

schema.description: Request body for updating a global picklist.

global_picklists.description: Array containing the global picklist to update.

items.description: Global picklist object with update details.

display_label.description: Updated display label of the global picklist.

description.description: Updated description of the global picklist.

pick_list_values_sorted_lexically.description: Indicates if picklist values should be sorted lexically.

pick_list_values.description: Array of picklist values to add, update, or delete. For updates, include id and fields to update. For deletion, include id and _delete: true. Only one value can be moved to unused or deleted per request.

type.description: Type of the picklist value (used or unused). Only one value can be moved to unused per request.

_delete.description: Set to true to delete this picklist value. Only one value can be deleted per request.

schema.description: Response object for successful update of a global picklist.

global_picklists.description: Array containing the updated global picklist response.

details.description: Details of the updated global picklist.

code.description: Error code indicating the type of validation or constraint failure.

delete.summary: Delete a global picklist by id
delete.description: Deletes a single global picklist identified by the path parameter \{id\}. This request is asynchronous: a 202 Accepted indicates deletion has been scheduled and the response contains a job_id in details. There is no status-check endpoint. A 400 Bad Request is returned for invalid id, deletion already scheduled, conversion in progress, association limits, or system-defined restrictions. When an error refers to a position in a multi-resource request/path, details.resource_path_index is provided.

202.description: Accepted — deletion scheduled. Response contains job_id in details. No status endpoint is provided.

schema.description: Response object for scheduled deletion of a global picklist.

global_picklists.description: Array containing the scheduled deletion response.

code.description: Response code indicating the operation was scheduled.

message.description: Human-readable message about the scheduled operation.

details.description: Additional details about the scheduled deletion.

job_id.description: Background job identifier for the scheduled deletion.

400.description: Bad Request — canonical error shapes (top-level or array-form). See examples for common cases. All variants include code, details, message, and status.

schema.description: Error response object for bad request containing code, message, details, and status.

code.description: Error code indicating the type of validation or constraint failure.

resource_path_index.description: Index in the request path or array indicating the failing resource position.

post.summary: Replace picklist values for a global picklist
post.description: Schedules or executes replacement of picklist values for the specified global picklist. You can specify either `id`, `display_value`, or both for `old_value` and `new_value`. If both are provided, the system validates them for consistency. The `from` (old) value can be non-existent in the current picklist, but the `to` (new) value must be in the **used** state of the picklist option.

schema.description: Request body for replacing picklist values in a global picklist.

replace_picklist_values.description: Array of picklist value replacement pairs.

items.description: Represents a single picklist value replacement pair.

old_value.description: The existing picklist value to be replaced.

new_value.description: The new picklist value that will replace the old one.

200.description: OK — Replacement completed immediately since the global set is not associated with any fields.

schema.description: Response object for immediate replacement completion.

code.description: Indicates that the replacement operation completed successfully.

details.description: Additional details about the replacement operation.

message.description: Human-readable message about the replacement operation.

202.description: Accepted — Replacement scheduled successfully for associated fields.

schema.description: response object for scheduled replacement of picklist values.

code.description: Indicates that the replacement operation has been scheduled.

details.description: Additional details about the replacement operation.

job_id.description: The ID of the scheduled replacement job.

message.description: Human-readable message about the replacement operation.

400.description: Bad Request — Invalid data, missing fields, ambiguity, or disallowed actions, or if limits exceeded.

oneOf.description: Error response with array of replacement operation errors.

replace_picklist_values.description: Array of replacement operation error details.

code.description: Error code indicating the type of validation or constraint failure.

api_name.description: name of the field causing the error.

json_path.description: JSON path to the field causing the error.

ambiguity_due_to.description: if both id and display_value are provided and they don't match

json_path.description: JSON path to the ambiguous field.

expected_fields.description: List of expected fields that are missing.

json_path.description: JSON path to where the field is expected.

limit_due_to.description: List of picklist values that caused the limit to be exceeded.

api_name.description: API name of the field causing limit issue.

json_path.description: JSON path to the field causing limit issue.

limit.description: The maximum allowed limit that has been exceeded. if this exists in details, limit_due_to will also be present.

details.description: Additional details about the top-level error.

get.summary: Get picklist values which is in replace scheduler
get.description: Retrieves picklist values that are currently being replaced through a replace scheduler for the given global picklist ID.

200.description: OK — Replace scheduler in progress. Returns values which are currently being replaced.

schema.description: Response object containing picklist values that are currently being replaced.

replaced_values.description: Array of picklist values that are currently being replaced.

items.description: Object representing a replaced picklist value.

204.description: No Content — No replace scheduler is currently in progress for the given picklist.

400.description: Bad Request — Invalid data or not allowed action.

schema.description: Error response object containing code, message, details, and status.

code.description: Error code indicating the failure reason. Possible values: INVALID_DATA, NOT_ALLOWED.

details.description: Details about the error, including the resource path index.

message.description: Describes the reason for the failure. Examples: 'the id given seems to be invalid' or 'global picklist deletion in progress.'

get.summary: To get the fields associated with a global picklist
get.description: Retrieves associations of picklist values with modules, fields, and layouts for a given global picklist ID.

parameters.name: include_inner_details
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of nested details to include (e.g., `module.plural_label,module.module_name,layouts.status`). Allowed values: module.plural_label, module.module_name, layouts.status.

items.description: Allowed values for including nested details in the response.

schema.description: Response object containing associations of global picklist fields and modules.

associations.description: List of associations between global picklist and fields/modules/layouts.

items.description: Association object for a global picklist field.

field.description: Field details associated with the global picklist.

module.description: Module details associated with the field.

layouts.description: List of layouts associated with the field.

info.description: Pagination and record info for associations response.

204.description: if there is no picklist field associated with the global picklist.

400.description: Bad Request — Invalid data or not allowed action.

schema.description: Error response object containing code, message, details, and status.

code.description: Error code indicating the failure reason. Possible values: INVALID_DATA, NOT_ALLOWED.

message.description: Describes the reason for the failure. Examples: 'the id given seems to be invalid' or 'global picklist deletion in progress.'

get.description: To the list of features like blueprints, workflows create, workflow convert, workflow task, ABM, etc in which a particular picklist value is used.

parameters.name: picklist_value_id
parameters.description: The ID of the picklist value to retrieve associations for.

pick_list_values_associations.description: Array of picklist value associations grouped by association type.

items.description: Association group for a specific type of feature.

resources.description: Array of resources associated with the picklist value.

oneOf.description: Blueprint details with module information.
Orchestration or workflow details with actions.

actions.description: Array of actions associated with the orchestration or workflow.

items.description: Action object with module and optional transition details.

items.description: Task or field update association object.

name.description: Name of the task or field update.

type.description: Type of association (field_update, task, blueprint, orchestration, workflow, etc.).

204.description: if the picklist value is not associated with any features.

400.description: Bad Request — Invalid data, missing parameters, or not allowed actions.

oneOf.description: Error response for invalid ID or not allowed actions with resource_path_index.

code.description: Error code indicating the type of error.

resource_path_index.description: Index in the resource path indicating the error location.

oneOf.description: Error response for missing required parameter.

oneOf.description: Error response for invalid parameter value.

Include.name: include
Include.description: Comma-separated list of related resources to include. Allowed values: "used_in_modules", "pick_list_values", "associated_fields_count". Use CSV encoding (style=form, explode=false) for multiple values, e.g. include=used_in_modules,pick_list_values.

items.description: Allowed values for including related resources in the response.

IncludeInnerDetails.name: include_inner_details
IncludeInnerDetails.description: Optional detailed expansion keys. Currently only allowed when `used_in_modules` is present in `include`. Allowed value: used_in_modules.plural_label.

PerPage.name: per_page
PerPage.description: Number of records per page. Default and maximum: 200.
